```json
{
  "markdown": "IntelliJ Platform Gradle Plugin (2.x) | IntelliJ Platform Plugin SDK \n\n# IntelliJ Platform Gradle Plugin (2.x)﻿\n\n[Edit page](https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-sdk-docs/edit/main/topics/appendix/tools/intellij_platform_gradle_plugin/tools_intellij_platform_gradle_plugin.md) Last modified: 27 January 2026\n\nCurrent Release: 2.13.1\n\nGitHub: [Releases \u0026 Changelog](https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-platform-gradle-plugin/releases), [Issue Tracker](https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-platform-gradle-plugin/issues)\n\nJetBrains Platform Forum: [Gradle Build Scripts](https://platform.jetbrains.com/c/intellij-platform/gradle-build-scripts/6) category\n\nThe IntelliJ Platform Gradle Plugin 2.x is a [Gradle](https://docs.gradle.org/current/userguide/userguide.html) plugin for building, testing, verifying, configuring environments, and publishing plugins for IntelliJ-based IDEs. It is the successor of [Gradle IntelliJ Plugin (1.x)](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-gradle-intellij-plugin.html) which is no longer under active development.\n\nLearn more about it in the [Release Announcement](https://blog.jetbrains.com/platform/2024/07/intellij-platform-gradle-plugin-2-0/).\n\n### tip\n\nFAQ\n\nSee [IntelliJ Platform Gradle Plugin – FAQ](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin-faq.html) and [Migrating from Gradle IntelliJ Plugin (1.x)](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin-migration.html).\n\n## Requirements﻿\n\nIntelliJ Platform Gradle Plugin 2.x requires the following minimal versions:\n\nIntelliJ Platform: 2022.3\n\nGradle: 8.13\n\nSee [the Gradle Installation guide](https://gradle.org/install/) on how to upgrade.\n\nJava Runtime: 17\n\nSee Gradle JVM in Settings | Build, Execution, Deployment | Build Tools | Gradle.\n\n### tip\n\nConsider using the [IntelliJ Platform Plugin Template](https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-platform-plugin-template) which additionally provides CI setup covered with GitHub Actions.\n\n## Setup﻿\n\n### note\n\nNote that the plugin has a new ID&#96;org.jetbrains.intellij.platform&#96;.\n\nTo apply the IntelliJ Platform Gradle Plugin to a project, add the following entry to the&#96;plugins&#96; block in the Gradle build file:\n\nKotlin\n\nGroovy\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;kotlin\nplugins {\n  id(\"org.jetbrains.intellij.platform\") version \"2.13.1\"\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;groovy\nplugins {\n  id 'org.jetbrains.intellij.platform' version '2.13.1'\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Using Snapshot Release﻿\n\n### Plugins﻿\n\nThe IntelliJ Platform Gradle Plugin consists of [plugins](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin-plugins.html) which can be applied depending on the purpose. By default, the [Platform](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin-plugins.html#platform) plugin (&#96;org.jetbrains.intellij.platform&#96;) should be applied to the main plugin project module.\n\nWhen working in a [Multi-Module Project Structure](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin.html#multi-module-project-structure) it is required to use [Module](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin-plugins.html#module) plugin (&#96;org.jetbrains.intellij.platform.module&#96;) instead of creating tasks and configurations specific to the main module only.\n\nThe [Settings](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin-plugins.html#settings) plugin (&#96;org.jetbrains.intellij.platform.settings&#96;) allows for adding plugin development related repositories right in the settings.gradle.kts file if project configuration involves [Dependency Resolution Management](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin.html#configuration.dependencyResolutionManagement).\n\n### Attaching Sources in the IDE﻿\n\nTo attach IntelliJ Platform sources in the IDE, the Download sources setting has to be enabled in IDE versions 2023.2 and later. This option respects the plugin's [downloadSources](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin-gradle-properties.html#downloadSources) property, which is enabled by default.\n\n2023.3+\n\n2023.2\n\nEarlier versions\n\nIn Settings | Advanced Settings enable option Download sources in section Build Tools. Gradle. Then invoke Reload All Gradle Projects action from the Gradle tool window.\n\nIn Settings | Build, Execution, Deployment | Build Tools | Gradle enable Download sources for dependencies. Then invoke the Reload All Gradle Projects action from the Gradle tool window.\n\nNo additional IDE settings are required.\n\nThe attaching sources operation in the IDE is handled by the Plugin DevKit plugin, thus it is recommended to always use the latest available IDE version.\n\nIf the opened compiled class has no sources available locally, the Plugin DevKit plugin will detect the relevant source coordinates and provide an action to Download IntelliJ Platform sources or Attach $API_NAME$ sources.\n\n## Configuration﻿\n\n### tip\n\nExploring Configuration Options\n\nAuto-completion, Quick Documentation, and other code insight features are available for many extension functions and properties.\n\n### Setting Up Repositories﻿\n\nAll IntelliJ Platform SDK artifacts are available via IntelliJ Maven repositories (see [IntelliJ Platform Artifacts Repositories](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/intellij-artifacts.html)), which exist in three variants:\n\nreleases\n\nsnapshots\n\nnightly (only selected artifacts)\n\n### tip\n\nIn most cases, the [defaultRepositories()](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin-repositories-extension.html#default-repositories) repository should be enough.\n\nExample:\n\nSetup Maven Central and [defaultRepositories()](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin-repositories-extension.html#default-repositories) repositories:\n\nKotlin\n\nGroovy\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;kotlin\nrepositories {\n  mavenCentral()\n\n  intellijPlatform {\n    defaultRepositories()\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;groovy\nrepositories {\n  mavenCentral()\n\n  intellijPlatform {\n    defaultRepositories()\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nExample #2:\n\nBuild a plugin against a release version of the IntelliJ Platform with dependency on a plugin from the JetBrains Marketplace:\n\nKotlin\n\nGroovy\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;kotlin\nrepositories {\n  mavenCentral()\n\n  intellijPlatform {\n    releases()\n    marketplace()\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;groovy\nrepositories {\n  mavenCentral()\n\n  intellijPlatform {\n    releases()\n    marketplace()\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nSee [Repositories Extension](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin-repositories-extension.html) on how to configure additional repositories.\n\n#### Dependency Resolution Management﻿\n\nTo access the IntelliJ Platform Gradle Plugin within the Gradle Settings file to use with&#96;dependencyResolutionManagement&#96;, add:\n\nKotlin\n\nGroovy\n\nsettings.gradle.kts\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;kotlin\nimport org.jetbrains.intellij.platform.gradle.extensions.intellijPlatform\n\nplugins {\n  id(\"org.jetbrains.intellij.platform.settings\") version \"2.13.1\"\n}\n\ndependencyResolutionManagement {\n  repositoriesMode = RepositoriesMode.FAIL_ON_PROJECT_REPOS\n\n  repositories {\n    mavenCentral()\n\n    intellijPlatform {\n      defaultRepositories()\n    }\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nsettings.gradle\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;groovy\nimport org.jetbrains.intellij.platform.gradle.extensions.intellijPlatform\n\nplugins {\n  id 'org.jetbrains.intellij.platform.settings' version '2.13.1'\n}\n\ndependencyResolutionManagement {\n  repositoriesMode = RepositoriesMode.FAIL_ON_PROJECT_REPOS\n\n  repositories {\n    mavenCentral()\n\n    intellijPlatform {\n      defaultRepositories()\n    }\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n#### Cache Redirector﻿\n\nSome repositories, by default, point to JetBrains Cache Redirector to provide better resource resolution. However, it is possible to use the direct repository URL, if available.\n\nTo switch off the default usage of JetBrains Cache Redirector, see the [useCacheRedirector](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin-gradle-properties.html#useCacheRedirector) Gradle property.\n\n### Setting Up IntelliJ Platform﻿\n\nDependencies and [repositories](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin.html#configuration.repositories) are handled using explicit entries within&#96;dependencies {}&#96; and&#96;repositories {}&#96; blocks in the Gradle build file.\n\nA minimum configuration for targeting IntelliJ IDEA 2024.3:\n\nKotlin\n\nGroovy\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;kotlin\nrepositories {\n  mavenCentral()\n\n  intellijPlatform {\n    defaultRepositories()\n  }\n}\n\ndependencies {\n  intellijPlatform {\n    intellijIdea(\"2024.3\")\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;groovy\nrepositories {\n  mavenCentral()\n\n  intellijPlatform {\n    defaultRepositories()\n  }\n}\n\ndependencies {\n  intellijPlatform {\n    intellijIdea('2024.3.6')\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe&#96;intellijIdea&#96; in the previous sample is one of the extension functions available for adding IntelliJ Platform dependencies to the project. See [Dependencies Extension](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin-dependencies-extension.html) on how to target other IDEs.\n\n### note\n\nWhen declaring a dependency on IntelliJ Platform, the IDE installer is resolved by default. IDE installers are OS-specific and contain [JetBrains Runtime (JBR)](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin-jetbrains-runtime.html) bundled, but have no EAP releases available.\n\nTo resolve EAP releases instead, opt-out from installer releases with&#96;useInstaller = false&#96; passed to the dependency helper.\n\nImportant: non-installer archives have no JetBrains Runtime (JBR) provided.\n\nRead more about [Target Versions](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin-dependencies-extension.html#target-versions).\n\n#### Parametrize IntelliJ Platform Dependency﻿\n\nAs a fallback,&#96;intellijPlatform&#96; extension can be used to allow dynamic configuration of the target platform, for example, via gradle.properties:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;none\nplatformType = IC\nplatformVersion = 2023.3\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe above Gradle properties can be referenced in the Gradle build file with:\n\nKotlin\n\nGroovy\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;kotlin\ndependencies {\n  intellijPlatform {\n    val type = providers.gradleProperty(\"platformType\")\n    val version = providers.gradleProperty(\"platformVersion\")\n\n    create(type, version)\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;groovy\ndependencies {\n  intellijPlatform {\n    def type = providers.gradleProperty('platformType')\n    def version = providers.gradleProperty('platformVersion')\n\n    create(type, version)\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe&#96;intellijPlatform&#96; helper accepts also the [IntelliJPlatformType](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin-types.html#IntelliJPlatformType) type:\n\nKotlin\n\nGroovy\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;kotlin\nimport org.jetbrains.intellij.platform.gradle.IntelliJPlatformType\n\ndependencies {\n  intellijPlatform {\n    val version = providers.gradleProperty(\"platformVersion\")\n\n    create(IntelliJPlatformType.IntellijIdeaUltimate, version)\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;groovy\nimport org.jetbrains.intellij.platform.gradle.IntelliJPlatformType\n\ndependencies {\n  intellijPlatform {\n    def version = providers.gradleProperty('platformVersion')\n\n    create(IntelliJPlatformType.IntellijIdeaUltimate, version)\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n#### Local IntelliJ Platform IDE Instance﻿\n\nIt is possible to refer to the locally available IntelliJ-based IDE using the&#96;local&#96; helper function:\n\nKotlin\n\nGroovy\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;kotlin\nrepositories {\n  intellijPlatform {\n    defaultRepositories()\n  }\n}\n\ndependencies {\n  intellijPlatform {\n    local(\"/Users/user/Applications/IntelliJ IDEA Ultimate.app\")\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;groovy\nrepositories {\n  intellijPlatform {\n    defaultRepositories()\n  }\n}\n\ndependencies {\n  intellijPlatform {\n    local '/Users/user/Applications/IntelliJ IDEA Ultimate.app'\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### tip\n\nlocalPlatformArtifacts() and defaultRepositories()\n\nNote that unless using recommended default [defaultRepositories()](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin-repositories-extension.html#default-repositories), the [localPlatformArtifacts()](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin-repositories-extension.html#additional-repositories) entry needs to be added to the&#96;repositories {}&#96; block explicitly to use local dependencies (bundled plugins, local IDE, custom plugin repositories, etc.).\n\n### Setting Up Plugin Dependencies﻿\n\nTo specify a dependency on a plugin, it is important to distinguish bundled plugins from plugins available in JetBrains Marketplace.\n\nThe [Dependencies Extension](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin-dependencies-extension.html) provides a set of helpers to manage [plugin dependencies](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin-dependencies-extension.html#plugins):\n\nKotlin\n\nGroovy\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;kotlin\nrepositories {\n  intellijPlatform {\n    defaultRepositories()\n  }\n}\n\ndependencies {\n  intellijPlatform {\n    intellijIdea(\"2026.1\")\n\n    bundledPlugin(\"com.intellij.java\")\n    plugin(\"org.intellij.scala\", \"2024.1.4\")\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;groovy\nrepositories {\n  intellijPlatform {\n    defaultRepositories()\n  }\n}\n\ndependencies {\n  intellijPlatform {\n    intellijIdea '2026.1'\n\n    bundledPlugin 'com.intellij.java'\n    plugin 'org.intellij.scala', '2024.1.4'\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### tip\n\nlocalPlatformArtifacts() and defaultRepositories()\n\nNote that unless using recommended default [defaultRepositories()](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin-repositories-extension.html#default-repositories), the [localPlatformArtifacts()](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin-repositories-extension.html#additional-repositories) entry needs to be added to the&#96;repositories {}&#96; block explicitly to use local dependencies (bundled plugins, local IDE, custom plugin repositories, etc.).\n\n### Multi-Module Project Structure﻿\n\nWhen working on a complex plugin, it is often convenient to split the code base into multiple submodules. To avoid polluting submodules with tasks or configurations specific to the root module only (for example, tasks for signing, publishing, or running the plugin), a dedicated sub-plugin was introduced.\n\nThe root module of the IntelliJ-based plugin project must apply the main [Platform](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin-plugins.html#platform) plugin as follows:\n\nKotlin\n\nGroovy\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;kotlin\nplugins {\n  id(\"org.jetbrains.intellij.platform\") version \"2.13.1\"\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;groovy\nplugins {\n  id 'org.jetbrains.intellij.platform' version '2.13.1'\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nAny other included submodule must use the [Module](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin-plugins.html#module) plugin instead:\n\nKotlin\n\nGroovy\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;kotlin\nplugins {\n  id(\"org.jetbrains.intellij.platform.module\")\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;groovy\nplugins {\n  id 'org.jetbrains.intellij.platform.module'\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### note\n\nSomething missing?\n\nIf a topic is not covered in the above sections, let us know via the Was this page helpful? feedback form below or [other channels](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/getting-help.html#problems-with-the-guide).\n\nBe specific about the topics and reasons for adding them and leave your email in case we need more details. Thanks for your feedback!\n\nThanks for your feedback!\n\nWas this page helpful?\n\nYesNo",
  "url": "https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/tools-intellij-platform-gradle-plugin.html#dependenciesLocalPlatform"
}
```

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_

## 这个报错是什么意思？

日志里的核心是：

```text
Premature end of Content-Length delimited message body
(expected: 1,579,722,173; received: 1,206,648,832)
```

含义是：

- 服务器在响应头里声明：这次要下载的 `ideaIU-2026.1-win.zip` **总长度约 1.58GB**（`Content-Length`）。
- 实际连接在只收到 **约 1.21GB** 时就断了（网络抖动、超时、代理中断、磁盘写入失败等）。
- 因此这是一次 **不完整、无效的下载**，Gradle **不会**把它当成已缓存好的 IDE。

所以：**这次并没有“下载成功”**，只是下了一部分就失败了。

## 为什么再次运行又会重新下载？

- 完整文件没有进缓存（或缓存条目无效），下次执行 `runIde` 仍会 **重新向 CDN 请求** 同一个 zip。
- 若网络问题依旧，可能 **反复失败**；配置好代理或换网络后，往往一次完整下载成功，之后就会走本地缓存，不再重复下（除非清缓存或换版本）。

---

## 能否自己下载后放到指定目录？

可以。项目里已支持通过 **`platformLocalPath`** 指向 **本机已安装的 IDE 根目录**（下面要有 `bin`、`lib` 等），Gradle 会走官方文档里的 **`local(...)`** 依赖，**不再下载** 对应的 zip。

在 `gradle.properties` 里增加（路径按你机器改）：

```properties
platformLocalPath=C:/Program Files/JetBrains/IntelliJ IDEA 2026.1
```

或从官网/Toolbox **先完整安装** 再填该安装目录。测完后可注释或删掉这一行，恢复用 `platformVersion` 在线解析。

更细的说明（含报错解释、`verifyPlugin` 与本地路径的关系）已写在 [`docs/TESTING.md`](docs/TESTING.md) 的「指定 IDE 版本」一节里。

---

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_

你本地报错里 Gradle 实际去拉的就是下面这个地址（Windows、Ultimate/统一发行版、对应 `2026.1` 这条解析线时）：

**Windows（zip，与 Gradle 默认解析一致）**  
`https://download.jetbrains.com/idea/ideaIU-2026.1.win.zip`

同一目录下通常还有其它产物，例如（具体以 JetBrains 当前页面为准）：

- **macOS（常见为 `.dmg` 安装包）**：在 [下载页](https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/download/) 选 macOS 会得到带版本号的链接；Gradle 若走 zip，有时会类似 `ideaIU-2026.1.mac.zip`（以实际仓库为准）。
- **Linux**：常见为 `.tar.gz`，链接形态类似 `https://download.jetbrains.com/idea/ideaIU-2026.1.tar.gz`（以实际为准）。

说明：

1. **`ideaIU`** 表示 IntelliJ IDEA **Ultimate** 产品线；2025.3 起统一发行版在构件命名上仍常用 `ideaIU`。
2. **小版本号**：若 JetBrains 发布的是 `2026.1.1` 之类，文件名可能变成 `ideaIU-2026.1.1.win.zip`，需要以 [官方下载页](https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/download/) 或浏览器下载时看到的真实文件名为准。
3. **Community**：若仍要旧式 **IC** 包，版本与命名规则不同；你当前工程在 2025.3+ 已按统一发行版用 **IU**，与上面一致。

若要自己用浏览器下载后给 Gradle 用本地目录，下完 **安装或解压后的 IDE 根目录** 填到 `platformLocalPath` 即可，不必再让 Gradle 下同一个 zip。

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_

原因：从 IntelliJ 2024.3 起，JSON 语言支持从平台核心拆成独立的 bundled plugin；用 `-PplatformVersion=2024.3` 编译时，若未把 `com.intellij.modules.json` 加入 Gradle 的 `bundledPlugins`，`com.intellij.json.*` 不会出现在编译 classpath，导致 “Unresolved reference 'json'”。正在在 `build.gradle.kts` 中根据 `platformVersion` 自动加入该 bundled plugin。

## 原因说明

从 **IntelliJ 2024.3** 起，JSON 支持从**平台核心**里拆成了独立的 **bundled plugin**（`com.intellij.modules.json`）。  
你用 `-PplatformVersion=2024.3` 时，Gradle 只拉取 **IC 平台 SDK**，若**没有**再声明依赖这个 JSON 插件，编译时 **`com.intellij.json.*` 不会出现在 classpath** 上，于是 Kotlin 报一整片 **`Unresolved reference 'json'`**（其实是 `com.intellij.json` 包解析不到）。

默认用 **2024.1.7** 时，JSON 仍在老平台里，所以以前不显式加 bundled plugin 也能编过。

## 已做的修复

在 `build.gradle.kts` 里增加了判断：当使用**在线下载的 IDE**（未设置 `platformLocalPath`）且 **`platformVersion` ≥ 2024.3**（含 2025.x、2026.x）时，自动在 `bundledPlugins` 里加上 **`com.intellij.modules.json`**；**2024.1 / 2024.2** 仍不加，避免以前那种「找不到 bundled plugin」的错误。

请再执行：

```powershell
.\gradlew.bat runIde "-PplatformVersion=2024.3"
```

应能正常编译并启动。若仍有问题，把完整报错贴出来即可。

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_

## 失败原因（不是单纯「超时」）

这些用例里 **`AssertionFailedError` 出在 `assertEquals`（结果条数）**，多数是 **搜索路径与测试 JSON 结构不一致**，不是时间阈值问题。

1. **`root.prop1` 错误**  
   `createTestFiles` 生成的是根上的 `prop0`…`prop4`，**没有名为 `root` 的属性**。  
   `findKey` 要求路径第一段必须是某个文件里的**根键名**，因此 `"root"` 永远匹配不上，返回 **0 条**，和期望的 `fileCount` 不符。

2. **深层嵌套路径错误**  
   `createDeeplyNestedFile` 生成的是 `root` → `level1` → … → `level50`，正确路径应是  
   `root.level1.level2....level50`。  
   原先只有 `level1....level50`，**少了 `root.`**，同样得不到 1 条结果。

3. **大文件 `prop0` 冲突**  
   `warmUpJvm` 会创建大量只含 `prop0`…`prop2` 的文件；再搜 `prop0` 会命中**很多文件**，不再是 1 条。  
   已改为搜索 **`prop5000`**（仅大文件里有）。

4. **并发测试键名错误**  
   原先用 `root.prop1`…`root.prop10`，与数据结构不符；已改为 **`prop0`…`prop4`**（与 `keysPerFile = 5` 一致）。

5. **预热**  
   将预热里错误的 `root.prop1` 改为 `prop1`，并把重复次数 **100 → 20**，减轻污染、加快执行。

当前已在本地跑通 **`JsonSearchPerformanceTest` 与完整 `test` 任务**。请再执行：

```powershell
.\gradlew.bat test
```

确认你那边也通过即可。
